mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions to equities by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate is on a downward path.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, tight real policy should compress valuation multiples and weaken equity risk-taking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the sign of the return effect does not remain negative after controlling for CPIAUCSL persistence.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent level indicates weakening household income and labor-market demand, which should depress near-term earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if, after the trigger, the S&P 500 has positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences or if the short-horizon return response is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals stronger consumption and labor-income support, which should improve earnings breadth and push S&P 500 returns positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive-month changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not correspond to above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and utilization, which should raise cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive following positive INDPRO changes, or if the sign flips negative during repeated expansionary samples.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior plateau signals weakening housing demand and tighter interest-sensitive spending, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect is absent when rates remain elevated.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is still expanding while UNRATE is flat or falling, the economy is in a soft-landing configuration that supports earnings without forcing tighter policy, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined GDP growth and stable/unimproving unemployment do not lead to above-baseline 60-day returns, or if any 3-year rolling window shows no positive edge.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor slowdown, rising PAYEMS with flat UNRATE indicates payroll growth is losing quality and cannot sustain demand, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days once payroll gains decelerate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth decelerates below its 6-month average while UNRATE is unchanged or higher
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger fails to predict negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if payroll deceleration with stable unemployment instead correlates with positive returns.
